urn:contentItem:6JHS-8DS3-RRN7-S4GP-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ AUSTRALIAN WEALTH MANAGER PARTNERS WITH ORACLE AI TO BOOST EFFICIENCY

Colonial First State (CFS) is investing in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to transform its operations. The Australian wealth manager manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly a million Australians as of the 24th of March 2026.The move consolidates HR, ERP and EPM into a single, unified platform, driving streamlined processes and data-driven decision-making.Jeroen Buwalda, COO, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The rollout is expected to accelerate workflows and support strategic initiatives.Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, noted that CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving wealth-management landscape. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ STRATEGIC BANKING MERGER APPROVED: 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP AND MID PENN BANCORP

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026, with more than 80% of outstanding shares represented and over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Rory G. Ritrievi commented that the support for the transaction was tremendous, and 1st Colonial's Robert White said the merger will strengthen the bank's ability to serve customers and communities.The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and bolster its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey. After closing, Mid Penn is expected to reach about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026, with 59 retail locations in Harrisburg-based operations.The deal reflects ongoing consolidation in the regional banking sector as both banks prepare for the integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66, KINDER MORGAN BUILD WESTERN GATEWAY FUEL PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER PLEDGES

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The project targets a new cross-regional fuel corridor to alleviate reliance on limited California pipeline links.The plan would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets and will tie into Las Vegas through CALNEV. It contemplates constructing a Borger, Texas to Phoenix line and reversing the Colton, California to Phoenix segment to enable east-to-west transport into California. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

+ BACKLOG-DRIVEN GROWTH AT KINDER MORGAN INTERSECTS WITH INSIDER SALES CONCERNS

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan’s US$10 billion backlog, centered on natural gas, is positioned to benefit from AI-enabled data centers and LNG exports.The company reported Q4 2025 results with record net income and adjusted EBITDA, noting that about 96% of cash flows come from fee-based or hedged contracts.Insider activity has continued, with a senior terminal executive selling shares over the past year, while the company targets US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion in earnings by 2029, a roughly 3.9% annual growth trajectory subject to market conditions and asset-management execution.Fair-value estimates among analysts range from about US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, reflecting divergent views on the backlog's ability to translate into value.

+ WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE GAINS MOMENTUM, LINKING MIDWEST REFINERIES TO WESTERN MARKETS

The initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline has closed with strong market interest, and Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan say the response supports advancing the project. A second open season is planned for January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and expanded delivery options, including new destinations west of Colton through a joint tariff with SFPP, L.P. that reconfigures the Colton-Phoenix segment to support westbound flows.The Western Gateway includes a new Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona line and the reversal of the SFPP, L.P. Colton-Phoenix segment. The plan also reverses the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to feed midcontinent refineries and supply Western Gateway’s western deliveries.The project aims to connect Midwest refining capacity with growing demand in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing supply diversity and energy security across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHY-DKN3-RTX9-G0X1-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ CFS UNIFIES HR, ERP AND EPM WITH ORACLE AI SUITE

Colonial First State (CFS), a leading Australian wealth manager, is deploying Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to modernize its operations and enhance the employee experience. As of the 24th of March 2026, CFS manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians.The transformation consolidates systems into a unified, agile platform, expected to accelerate innovation and improve operational consistency as CFS seeks to meet client expectations.Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The implementation is seen as a catalyst for smarter workflows, better decision-making and enabling CFS to pursue strategic priorities.The integration of Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) has unified HR operations, streamlining onboarding and workforce data insights, empowering HR leaders with a single, reliable view of data to support development initiatives.

+ SHAREHOLDERS BACK 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP'S MERGER WITH MID PENN BANCORP, EXPANDING REGIONAL FOOTPRINT

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has won shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026. With more than 80% of outstanding shares represented, the vote passed decisively, with over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Chair, President and CEO Rory G. Ritrievi said the level of support for this transaction was tremendous, underscoring strong industry and investor backing for the combination.1st Colonial's President and CEO Robert White noted the merger will enhance the company's ability to serve customers and communities, while positioning Mid Penn to extend its footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and deepen its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey.Upon closing, Mid Penn is projected to have about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026. Mid Penn Bancorp, headquartered in Harrisburg, operates 59 retail locations and offers a broad suite of financial products and services, reinforcing its role in the regional banking landscape.The transaction aligns with a broader trend of consolidation in the regional banking sector, as institutions seek scale to improve market presence and operational efficiency while integration proceeds.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) PROJECTS U.S. GAS MARKET TO GROW ABOUT 19 BCF/D, BACKLOG SUPPORTS LNG INFRASTRUCTURE

On 5th May 2026, Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) discussed how geopolitical tensions are lifting demand for U.S. LNG and related pipeline capacity. President Dax Sanders noted that U.S. producers remain disciplined while watching long-term price signals that could prompt higher output, with energy security concerns supporting Permian Basin volumes.Sanders pointed to geopolitics boosting demand for LNG and pipeline capacity, citing a reduction of about 2-3 Bcf/d in Middle Eastern liquefaction capacity and industry forecasts suggesting growth of around 19 Bcf/d in the U.S. gas market over the next four to five years, much of it from LNG. Kinder Morgan moves roughly 40% of U.S. gas and liquefaction feed.To capitalize on this demand, the company Posts a $10.1 billion sanctioned backlog of projects, including the under-construction Trident Pipeline, which will initially transport 2 Bcf/d of gas and has potential for expansion. The Monument Pipeline was completed for approximately $500 million, strengthening the companys regional footprint.Wood Mackenzie estimates point to substantial growth in the U.S. gas market, with Kinder Morgans internal projections running even more optimistic. With an extensive network moving a significant portion of the nation’s gas, the company is positioned to meet rising LNG demand as geopolitical dynamics shift supply chains and buyers sourcing strategies.

+ PHILLIPS 66 (NYSE:PSX) AND KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) MOVE FORWARD ON WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER COMMITMENTS

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The collaboration reflects a shared objective to strengthen West Coast fuel resilience amid evolving regulatory and market conditions.The Western Gateway Pipeline would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets, addressing growing demand and reducing exposure to supply disruptions. The project would also connect to Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing interregional flow of refined products.A new Borger, Texas to Phoenix line is planned, and the Colton, California to Phoenix leg would be reversed to enable east-to-west fuel movement. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. The parties have not disclosed total project costs, and service is expected to commence in mid-2029.

+ KINDER MORGAN SEES CASH-FLOW STRENGTH BACKED BY BACKLOG, AMID INSIDER-STOCK ACTIVITY

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan is balancing a robust project backlog with ongoing insider stock sales, creating a nuanced narrative for investors. The backlog, pegged at about US$10 billion and focused mainly on natural gas, positions the company to benefit from rising demand from AI-driven data centers and LNG exports.The company says the pipeline’s structure yields contracted, infrastructure-like cash flows that underpin a stable base. In its Q4 2025 results, Kinder Morgan posted record net income and adjusted EBITDA, with about 96% of cash flows coming from fee-based or hedged contracts.Nonetheless, insider activity remains a point of concern. Filings show that a senior terminal executive has continued to sell shares over the past year, prompting questions about insider confidence in growth prospects and the sustainability of a capital-intensive model amid a sizable net debt.Looking ahead, Kinder Morgan projects US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion of earnings by 2029, implying a roughly 3.9% annual growth pace, if conditions stay supportive. Analysts offer a wide range of fair-value estimates, from US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, underscoring divergent views on how the backlog could translate into value.In sum, the company’s combination of a large backlog and fee-based cash flows presents a compelling case for some investors, while insider sales and leverage inject caution for others as market conditions evolve.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHK-P5P3-RTB0-B1C2-00000-00Salem County eyed as site for nuclear reactor, governor says Sherrill pushes nuclear power to cut electricity costs as lawmakers question grid strain, data center demand. 2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:00:05ZLexisNexisAPg. 001Salem County eyed as site for nuclear reactor, governor says Sherrill pushes nuclear power to cut electricity costs as lawmakers question grid strain, data center demand. Jelani Gibson - NJ.com

New Jersey officials are beginning to consider potential locations for new nuclear facilities as the state grapples with higher electricity prices and growing strain on the power grid.

Gov. Mikie Sherrill said the state is looking at sites, including ones in Salem County near the existing Salem Nuclear Power Plant on the Delaware Bay in Lower Alloways Creek Township.

The area is one of only six preapproved sites in the nation designated for new nuclear power projects, Sherrill told NJ.com last week in an interview about her first 100 days in office.

"It was always anticipated we'd build another nuclear reactor down there," Sherrill said.

The governor said she doesn't anticipate problems with the local community opposing nuclear power in its area.

"We don't have a NIMBY issue," Sherrill said of the location. "It's an incredibly safe plant."

Salem County, with about 65,000 residents, is New Jersey's least-populated county.

Sherrill recently lifted regulations that had effectively barred the development of new nuclear power plants in the state. That opens the door for future projects.

The move drew support from some Democrats but also prompted criticism from environmental advocates.

Acting State Environmental Commissioner Ed Potosnak told legislators during a state budget hearing in Trenton last week that state officials are moving forward with screening possible sites for new nuclear power facilities.

The new nuclear sites could help meet increasing demand for utilities fueled by a surge in artificial intelligence data centers. Facilities that provide computing power for AI consume large amounts of electricity and water. Proposals for AI data centers have become increasingly controversial in towns across the state.

Nuclear power is often cited by lawmakers as a stable, carbon-neutral energy source that could help meet the demand created by AI data centers. Potosnak was questioned at last week's budget hearing about how the administration views the overlap between the two issues.

State Senate Budget Chair Paul Sarlo, D Bergen, said he has supported data centers in the past but is growing more cautious.

"I've been a big supporter of data centers, but I'm starting to -- putting on my engineering hat here -- I'm starting to take a really hard look at the impact these have," Sarlo said.

Business groups pushed back against criticism of the facilities. The New Jersey Business and Industry Association said data centers have helped accelerate investment in renewable energy and in upgrades to the electric grid.

"Data centers are helping modernize outdated electrical grids by advancing sustainability and accelerating the shift to renewable energy, helping to position our communities for the next generation of energy efficiency," said NJBIA analyst Jack Ramirez.

Environmental critics have raised concerns about nuclear waste disposal, arguing that there is no permanent solution for spent nuclear fuel. Waste is currently stored in large containment systems at power plant sites, a practice critics say shifts the burden to future generations.

Lawmakers also pressed the administration on nuclear safety and oversight. State Sen. Andrew Zwicker, D-Somerset, questioned how security would be managed at any new plants and whether state protections would remain strong if federal standards were loosened.

Potosnak said the state would closely examine any changes at the federal level.

"I think it merits close consideration," he said.

He also emphasized the safety record of New Jersey's existing nuclear facilities.

"I've had a chance to tour our existing nuclear facilities and their attention to safety and detail is truly impressive, and I don't want to have anything have a negative impact on that at all," Zwicker said.

Support for expanding nuclear power has emerged across party lines. State Sen. Carmen Amato, R Ocean, has also said that additional nuclear generation could help relieve pressure on the state's electric grid.

Sherrill has appointed a task force, led by the state Department of Environmental Protection, to address the nuclear initiative. Amato said he expects the group to focus on streamlining the development process.

"As we know, nuclear takes a while to construct and it's also very expensive, so I look forward to the task force meeting and trying to speed that up," he said.

Locations near the Salem Nuclear Power Plant on the Delaware Bay in Lower Alloways Creek Township are being considered. File

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ CFS UNIFIES HR, ERP AND EPM WITH ORACLE AI SUITE

Colonial First State (CFS), a leading Australian wealth manager, is deploying Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to modernize its operations and enhance the employee experience. As of the 24th of March 2026, CFS manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians.The transformation consolidates systems into a unified, agile platform, expected to accelerate innovation and improve operational consistency as CFS seeks to meet client expectations.Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The implementation is seen as a catalyst for smarter workflows, better decision-making and enabling CFS to pursue strategic priorities.The integration of Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) has unified HR operations, streamlining onboarding and workforce data insights, empowering HR leaders with a single, reliable view of data to support development initiatives.

+ SHAREHOLDERS BACK 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP'S MERGER WITH MID PENN BANCORP, EXPANDING REGIONAL FOOTPRINT

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has won shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026. With more than 80% of outstanding shares represented, the vote passed decisively, with over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Chair, President and CEO Rory G. Ritrievi said the level of support for this transaction was tremendous, underscoring strong industry and investor backing for the combination.1st Colonial's President and CEO Robert White noted the merger will enhance the company's ability to serve customers and communities, while positioning Mid Penn to extend its footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and deepen its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey.Upon closing, Mid Penn is projected to have about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026. Mid Penn Bancorp, headquartered in Harrisburg, operates 59 retail locations and offers a broad suite of financial products and services, reinforcing its role in the regional banking landscape.The transaction aligns with a broader trend of consolidation in the regional banking sector, as institutions seek scale to improve market presence and operational efficiency while integration proceeds.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66 (NYSE:PSX) AND KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) MOVE FORWARD ON WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER COMMITMENTS

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The collaboration reflects a shared objective to strengthen West Coast fuel resilience amid evolving regulatory and market conditions.The Western Gateway Pipeline would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets, addressing growing demand and reducing exposure to supply disruptions. The project would also connect to Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing interregional flow of refined products.A new Borger, Texas to Phoenix line is planned, and the Colton, California to Phoenix leg would be reversed to enable east-to-west fuel movement. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. The parties have not disclosed total project costs, and service is expected to commence in mid-2029.

+ KINDER MORGAN SEES CASH-FLOW STRENGTH BACKED BY BACKLOG, AMID INSIDER-STOCK ACTIVITY

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan is balancing a robust project backlog with ongoing insider stock sales, creating a nuanced narrative for investors. The backlog, pegged at about US$10 billion and focused mainly on natural gas, positions the company to benefit from rising demand from AI-driven data centers and LNG exports.The company says the pipeline’s structure yields contracted, infrastructure-like cash flows that underpin a stable base. In its Q4 2025 results, Kinder Morgan posted record net income and adjusted EBITDA, with about 96% of cash flows coming from fee-based or hedged contracts.Nonetheless, insider activity remains a point of concern. Filings show that a senior terminal executive has continued to sell shares over the past year, prompting questions about insider confidence in growth prospects and the sustainability of a capital-intensive model amid a sizable net debt.Looking ahead, Kinder Morgan projects US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion of earnings by 2029, implying a roughly 3.9% annual growth pace, if conditions stay supportive. Analysts offer a wide range of fair-value estimates, from US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, underscoring divergent views on how the backlog could translate into value.In sum, the company’s combination of a large backlog and fee-based cash flows presents a compelling case for some investors, while insider sales and leverage inject caution for others as market conditions evolve.

+ OPEN SEASON SUCCESS SPURS NEXT PHASE FOR WESTERN GATEWAY REFINED-PRODUCTS CORRIDOR

Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan announced that the initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline attracted strong market interest and solid shipper commitments, reinforcing demand for added refined-products transportation capacity into the Southwest and California. The partners said the response warrants moving forward with a second open season in January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and to introduce expanded delivery options. The next period will bring new destination points west of Colton, California, made available through a joint tariff with an SFPP, L.P. segment that will be reconfigured to support westbound flows between Watson and Colton, ensuring shippers can reach the Los Angeles market more directly.The Western Gateway project comprises a new-build segment from Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona and the integration of Kinder Morgan's existing SFPP, L.P. pipeline from Colton to Phoenix. As part of the plan, the SFPP, L.P. segment will be reversed to move refined products from east to west into California, aligning with the project’s objective to shift flows toward West Coast demand centers. The system will draw supply from Borger and El Paso, Texas, connecting midcontinent refining with Southwest demand centers, and will include reversing the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to route midcontinent products into Western Gateway.Once complete, the initiative is designed to create a robust transport corridor linking Midwest refining with growth markets in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas, via the CALNEV extension. The project seeks to diversify supply, improve energy security, and support sustained demand growth across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ AUSTRALIAN WEALTH MANAGER PARTNERS WITH ORACLE AI TO BOOST EFFICIENCY

Colonial First State (CFS) is investing in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to transform its operations. The Australian wealth manager manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly a million Australians as of the 24th of March 2026.The move consolidates HR, ERP and EPM into a single, unified platform, driving streamlined processes and data-driven decision-making.Jeroen Buwalda, COO, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The rollout is expected to accelerate workflows and support strategic initiatives.Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, noted that CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving wealth-management landscape. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ STRATEGIC BANKING MERGER APPROVED: 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP AND MID PENN BANCORP

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026, with more than 80% of outstanding shares represented and over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Rory G. Ritrievi commented that the support for the transaction was tremendous, and 1st Colonial's Robert White said the merger will strengthen the bank's ability to serve customers and communities.The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and bolster its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey. After closing, Mid Penn is expected to reach about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026, with 59 retail locations in Harrisburg-based operations.The deal reflects ongoing consolidation in the regional banking sector as both banks prepare for the integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66, KINDER MORGAN BUILD WESTERN GATEWAY FUEL PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER PLEDGES

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The project targets a new cross-regional fuel corridor to alleviate reliance on limited California pipeline links.The plan would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets and will tie into Las Vegas through CALNEV. It contemplates constructing a Borger, Texas to Phoenix line and reversing the Colton, California to Phoenix segment to enable east-to-west transport into California. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

+ BACKLOG-DRIVEN GROWTH AT KINDER MORGAN INTERSECTS WITH INSIDER SALES CONCERNS

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan’s US$10 billion backlog, centered on natural gas, is positioned to benefit from AI-enabled data centers and LNG exports.The company reported Q4 2025 results with record net income and adjusted EBITDA, noting that about 96% of cash flows come from fee-based or hedged contracts.Insider activity has continued, with a senior terminal executive selling shares over the past year, while the company targets US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion in earnings by 2029, a roughly 3.9% annual growth trajectory subject to market conditions and asset-management execution.Fair-value estimates among analysts range from about US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, reflecting divergent views on the backlog's ability to translate into value.

+ WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE GAINS MOMENTUM, LINKING MIDWEST REFINERIES TO WESTERN MARKETS

The initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline has closed with strong market interest, and Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan say the response supports advancing the project. A second open season is planned for January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and expanded delivery options, including new destinations west of Colton through a joint tariff with SFPP, L.P. that reconfigures the Colton-Phoenix segment to support westbound flows.The Western Gateway includes a new Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona line and the reversal of the SFPP, L.P. Colton-Phoenix segment. The plan also reverses the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to feed midcontinent refineries and supply Western Gateway’s western deliveries.The project aims to connect Midwest refining capacity with growing demand in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing supply diversity and energy security across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHK-P5P3-RTB0-B190-00000-00Salem County eyed as site for nuclear reactor, governor says Sherrill pushes nuclear power to cut electricity costs as lawmakers question grid strain, data center demand. 2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:00:05ZLexisNexisAPg. 001Salem County eyed as site for nuclear reactor, governor says Sherrill pushes nuclear power to cut electricity costs as lawmakers question grid strain, data center demand. Jelani Gibson - NJ.com

New Jersey officials are beginning to consider potential locations for new nuclear facilities as the state grapples with higher electricity prices and growing strain on the power grid.

Gov. Mikie Sherrill said the state is looking at sites, including ones in Salem County near the existing Salem Nuclear Power Plant on the Delaware Bay in Lower Alloways Creek Township.

The area is one of only six preapproved sites in the nation designated for new nuclear power projects, Sherrill told NJ.com last week in an interview about her first 100 days in office.

"It was always anticipated we'd build another nuclear reactor down there," Sherrill said.

The governor said she doesn't anticipate problems with the local community opposing nuclear power in its area.

"We don't have a NIMBY issue," Sherrill said of the location. "It's an incredibly safe plant."

Salem County, with about 65,000 residents, is New Jersey's least-populated county.

Sherrill recently lifted regulations that had effectively barred the development of new nuclear power plants in the state. That opens the door for future projects.

The move drew support from some Democrats but also prompted criticism from environmental advocates.

Acting State Environmental Commissioner Ed Potosnak told legislators during a state budget hearing in Trenton last week that state officials are moving forward with screening possible sites for new nuclear power facilities.

The new nuclear sites could help meet increasing demand for utilities fueled by a surge in artificial intelligence data centers. Facilities that provide computing power for AI consume large amounts of electricity and water. Proposals for AI data centers have become increasingly controversial in towns across the state.

Nuclear power is often cited by lawmakers as a stable, carbon-neutral energy source that could help meet the demand created by AI data centers. Potosnak was questioned at last week's budget hearing about how the administration views the overlap between the two issues.

State Senate Budget Chair Paul Sarlo, D Bergen, said he has supported data centers in the past but is growing more cautious.

"I've been a big supporter of data centers, but I'm starting to -- putting on my engineering hat here -- I'm starting to take a really hard look at the impact these have," Sarlo said.

Business groups pushed back against criticism of the facilities. The New Jersey Business and Industry Association said data centers have helped accelerate investment in renewable energy and in upgrades to the electric grid.

"Data centers are helping modernize outdated electrical grids by advancing sustainability and accelerating the shift to renewable energy, helping to position our communities for the next generation of energy efficiency," said NJBIA analyst Jack Ramirez.

Environmental critics have raised concerns about nuclear waste disposal, arguing that there is no permanent solution for spent nuclear fuel. Waste is currently stored in large containment systems at power plant sites, a practice critics say shifts the burden to future generations.

Lawmakers also pressed the administration on nuclear safety and oversight. State Sen. Andrew Zwicker, D-Somerset, questioned how security would be managed at any new plants and whether state protections would remain strong if federal standards were loosened.

Potosnak said the state would closely examine any changes at the federal level.

"I think it merits close consideration," he said.

He also emphasized the safety record of New Jersey's existing nuclear facilities.

"I've had a chance to tour our existing nuclear facilities and their attention to safety and detail is truly impressive, and I don't want to have anything have a negative impact on that at all," Zwicker said.

Support for expanding nuclear power has emerged across party lines. State Sen. Carmen Amato, R Ocean, has also said that additional nuclear generation could help relieve pressure on the state's electric grid.

Sherrill has appointed a task force, led by the state Department of Environmental Protection, to address the nuclear initiative. Amato said he expects the group to focus on streamlining the development process.

"As we know, nuclear takes a while to construct and it's also very expensive, so I look forward to the task force meeting and trying to speed that up," he said.

Locations near the Salem Nuclear Power Plant on the Delaware Bay in Lower Alloways Creek Township are being considered. File

"I think it merits close consideration," he said.

He also emphasized the safety record of New Jersey's existing nuclear facilities.

"I've had a chance to tour our existing nuclear facilities and their attention to safety and detail is truly impressive, and I don't want to have anything have a negative impact on that at all," Zwicker said.

Support for expanding nuclear power has emerged across party lines. State Sen. Carmen Amato, R Ocean, has also said that additional nuclear generation could help relieve pressure on the state's electric grid.

Sherrill has appointed a task force, led by the state Department of Environmental Protection, to address the nuclear initiative. Amato said he expects the group to focus on streamlining the development process.

"As we know, nuclear takes a while to construct and it's also very expensive, so I look forward to the task force meeting and trying to speed that up," he said.

Locations near the Salem Nuclear Power Plant on the Delaware Bay in Lower Alloways Creek Township are being considered. File

urn:contentItem:6JHH-G7C3-SFSX-M17S-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ CFS UNIFIES HR, ERP AND EPM WITH ORACLE AI SUITE

Colonial First State (CFS), a leading Australian wealth manager, is deploying Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to modernize its operations and enhance the employee experience. As of the 24th of March 2026, CFS manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians.The transformation consolidates systems into a unified, agile platform, expected to accelerate innovation and improve operational consistency as CFS seeks to meet client expectations.Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The implementation is seen as a catalyst for smarter workflows, better decision-making and enabling CFS to pursue strategic priorities.The integration of Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) has unified HR operations, streamlining onboarding and workforce data insights, empowering HR leaders with a single, reliable view of data to support development initiatives.

+ SHAREHOLDERS BACK 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP'S MERGER WITH MID PENN BANCORP, EXPANDING REGIONAL FOOTPRINT

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has won shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026. With more than 80% of outstanding shares represented, the vote passed decisively, with over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Chair, President and CEO Rory G. Ritrievi said the level of support for this transaction was tremendous, underscoring strong industry and investor backing for the combination.1st Colonial's President and CEO Robert White noted the merger will enhance the company's ability to serve customers and communities, while positioning Mid Penn to extend its footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and deepen its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey.Upon closing, Mid Penn is projected to have about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026. Mid Penn Bancorp, headquartered in Harrisburg, operates 59 retail locations and offers a broad suite of financial products and services, reinforcing its role in the regional banking landscape.The transaction aligns with a broader trend of consolidation in the regional banking sector, as institutions seek scale to improve market presence and operational efficiency while integration proceeds.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66 (NYSE:PSX) AND KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) MOVE FORWARD ON WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER COMMITMENTS

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The collaboration reflects a shared objective to strengthen West Coast fuel resilience amid evolving regulatory and market conditions.The Western Gateway Pipeline would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets, addressing growing demand and reducing exposure to supply disruptions. The project would also connect to Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing interregional flow of refined products.A new Borger, Texas to Phoenix line is planned, and the Colton, California to Phoenix leg would be reversed to enable east-to-west fuel movement. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. The parties have not disclosed total project costs, and service is expected to commence in mid-2029.

+ KINDER MORGAN SEES CASH-FLOW STRENGTH BACKED BY BACKLOG, AMID INSIDER-STOCK ACTIVITY

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan is balancing a robust project backlog with ongoing insider stock sales, creating a nuanced narrative for investors. The backlog, pegged at about US$10 billion and focused mainly on natural gas, positions the company to benefit from rising demand from AI-driven data centers and LNG exports.The company says the pipeline’s structure yields contracted, infrastructure-like cash flows that underpin a stable base. In its Q4 2025 results, Kinder Morgan posted record net income and adjusted EBITDA, with about 96% of cash flows coming from fee-based or hedged contracts.Nonetheless, insider activity remains a point of concern. Filings show that a senior terminal executive has continued to sell shares over the past year, prompting questions about insider confidence in growth prospects and the sustainability of a capital-intensive model amid a sizable net debt.Looking ahead, Kinder Morgan projects US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion of earnings by 2029, implying a roughly 3.9% annual growth pace, if conditions stay supportive. Analysts offer a wide range of fair-value estimates, from US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, underscoring divergent views on how the backlog could translate into value.In sum, the company’s combination of a large backlog and fee-based cash flows presents a compelling case for some investors, while insider sales and leverage inject caution for others as market conditions evolve.

+ OPEN SEASON SUCCESS SPURS NEXT PHASE FOR WESTERN GATEWAY REFINED-PRODUCTS CORRIDOR

Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan announced that the initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline attracted strong market interest and solid shipper commitments, reinforcing demand for added refined-products transportation capacity into the Southwest and California. The partners said the response warrants moving forward with a second open season in January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and to introduce expanded delivery options. The next period will bring new destination points west of Colton, California, made available through a joint tariff with an SFPP, L.P. segment that will be reconfigured to support westbound flows between Watson and Colton, ensuring shippers can reach the Los Angeles market more directly.The Western Gateway project comprises a new-build segment from Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona and the integration of Kinder Morgan's existing SFPP, L.P. pipeline from Colton to Phoenix. As part of the plan, the SFPP, L.P. segment will be reversed to move refined products from east to west into California, aligning with the project’s objective to shift flows toward West Coast demand centers. The system will draw supply from Borger and El Paso, Texas, connecting midcontinent refining with Southwest demand centers, and will include reversing the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to route midcontinent products into Western Gateway.Once complete, the initiative is designed to create a robust transport corridor linking Midwest refining with growth markets in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas, via the CALNEV extension. The project seeks to diversify supply, improve energy security, and support sustained demand growth across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHB-9XG3-RSRS-S4Y7-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ AUSTRALIAN WEALTH MANAGER PARTNERS WITH ORACLE AI TO BOOST EFFICIENCY

Colonial First State (CFS) is investing in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to transform its operations. The Australian wealth manager manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly a million Australians as of the 24th of March 2026.The move consolidates HR, ERP and EPM into a single, unified platform, driving streamlined processes and data-driven decision-making.Jeroen Buwalda, COO, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The rollout is expected to accelerate workflows and support strategic initiatives.Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, noted that CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving wealth-management landscape. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ STRATEGIC BANKING MERGER APPROVED: 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP AND MID PENN BANCORP

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026, with more than 80% of outstanding shares represented and over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Rory G. Ritrievi commented that the support for the transaction was tremendous, and 1st Colonial's Robert White said the merger will strengthen the bank's ability to serve customers and communities.The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and bolster its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey. After closing, Mid Penn is expected to reach about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026, with 59 retail locations in Harrisburg-based operations.The deal reflects ongoing consolidation in the regional banking sector as both banks prepare for the integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66, KINDER MORGAN BUILD WESTERN GATEWAY FUEL PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER PLEDGES

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The project targets a new cross-regional fuel corridor to alleviate reliance on limited California pipeline links.The plan would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets and will tie into Las Vegas through CALNEV. It contemplates constructing a Borger, Texas to Phoenix line and reversing the Colton, California to Phoenix segment to enable east-to-west transport into California. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

+ BACKLOG-DRIVEN GROWTH AT KINDER MORGAN INTERSECTS WITH INSIDER SALES CONCERNS

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan’s US$10 billion backlog, centered on natural gas, is positioned to benefit from AI-enabled data centers and LNG exports.The company reported Q4 2025 results with record net income and adjusted EBITDA, noting that about 96% of cash flows come from fee-based or hedged contracts.Insider activity has continued, with a senior terminal executive selling shares over the past year, while the company targets US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion in earnings by 2029, a roughly 3.9% annual growth trajectory subject to market conditions and asset-management execution.Fair-value estimates among analysts range from about US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, reflecting divergent views on the backlog's ability to translate into value.

+ WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE GAINS MOMENTUM, LINKING MIDWEST REFINERIES TO WESTERN MARKETS

The initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline has closed with strong market interest, and Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan say the response supports advancing the project. A second open season is planned for January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and expanded delivery options, including new destinations west of Colton through a joint tariff with SFPP, L.P. that reconfigures the Colton-Phoenix segment to support westbound flows.The Western Gateway includes a new Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona line and the reversal of the SFPP, L.P. Colton-Phoenix segment. The plan also reverses the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to feed midcontinent refineries and supply Western Gateway’s western deliveries.The project aims to connect Midwest refining capacity with growing demand in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing supply diversity and energy security across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JH9-GXP3-S940-Y346-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT NEWS FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT NEWS FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ CFS UNIFIES HR, ERP AND EPM WITH ORACLE AI SUITE

Colonial First State (CFS), a leading Australian wealth manager, is deploying Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to modernize its operations and enhance the employee experience. As of the 24th of March 2026, CFS manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians.The transformation consolidates systems into a unified, agile platform, expected to accelerate innovation and improve operational consistency as CFS seeks to meet client expectations.Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The implementation is seen as a catalyst for smarter workflows, better decision-making and enabling CFS to pursue strategic priorities.The integration of Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) has unified HR operations, streamlining onboarding and workforce data insights, empowering HR leaders with a single, reliable view of data to support development initiatives.

+ SHAREHOLDERS BACK 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP'S MERGER WITH MID PENN BANCORP, EXPANDING REGIONAL FOOTPRINT

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has won shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026. With more than 80% of outstanding shares represented, the vote passed decisively, with over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Chair, President and CEO Rory G. Ritrievi said the level of support for this transaction was tremendous, underscoring strong industry and investor backing for the combination.1st Colonial's President and CEO Robert White noted the merger will enhance the company's ability to serve customers and communities, while positioning Mid Penn to extend its footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and deepen its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey.Upon closing, Mid Penn is projected to have about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026. Mid Penn Bancorp, headquartered in Harrisburg, operates 59 retail locations and offers a broad suite of financial products and services, reinforcing its role in the regional banking landscape.The transaction aligns with a broader trend of consolidation in the regional banking sector, as institutions seek scale to improve market presence and operational efficiency while integration proceeds.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66 (NYSE:PSX) AND KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) MOVE FORWARD ON WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER COMMITMENTS

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The collaboration reflects a shared objective to strengthen West Coast fuel resilience amid evolving regulatory and market conditions.The Western Gateway Pipeline would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets, addressing growing demand and reducing exposure to supply disruptions. The project would also connect to Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing interregional flow of refined products.A new Borger, Texas to Phoenix line is planned, and the Colton, California to Phoenix leg would be reversed to enable east-to-west fuel movement. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. The parties have not disclosed total project costs, and service is expected to commence in mid-2029.

+ KINDER MORGAN SEES CASH-FLOW STRENGTH BACKED BY BACKLOG, AMID INSIDER-STOCK ACTIVITY

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan is balancing a robust project backlog with ongoing insider stock sales, creating a nuanced narrative for investors. The backlog, pegged at about US$10 billion and focused mainly on natural gas, positions the company to benefit from rising demand from AI-driven data centers and LNG exports.The company says the pipeline’s structure yields contracted, infrastructure-like cash flows that underpin a stable base. In its Q4 2025 results, Kinder Morgan posted record net income and adjusted EBITDA, with about 96% of cash flows coming from fee-based or hedged contracts.Nonetheless, insider activity remains a point of concern. Filings show that a senior terminal executive has continued to sell shares over the past year, prompting questions about insider confidence in growth prospects and the sustainability of a capital-intensive model amid a sizable net debt.Looking ahead, Kinder Morgan projects US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion of earnings by 2029, implying a roughly 3.9% annual growth pace, if conditions stay supportive. Analysts offer a wide range of fair-value estimates, from US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, underscoring divergent views on how the backlog could translate into value.In sum, the company’s combination of a large backlog and fee-based cash flows presents a compelling case for some investors, while insider sales and leverage inject caution for others as market conditions evolve.

+ OPEN SEASON SUCCESS SPURS NEXT PHASE FOR WESTERN GATEWAY REFINED-PRODUCTS CORRIDOR

Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan announced that the initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline attracted strong market interest and solid shipper commitments, reinforcing demand for added refined-products transportation capacity into the Southwest and California. The partners said the response warrants moving forward with a second open season in January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and to introduce expanded delivery options. The next period will bring new destination points west of Colton, California, made available through a joint tariff with an SFPP, L.P. segment that will be reconfigured to support westbound flows between Watson and Colton, ensuring shippers can reach the Los Angeles market more directly.The Western Gateway project comprises a new-build segment from Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona and the integration of Kinder Morgan's existing SFPP, L.P. pipeline from Colton to Phoenix. As part of the plan, the SFPP, L.P. segment will be reversed to move refined products from east to west into California, aligning with the project’s objective to shift flows toward West Coast demand centers. The system will draw supply from Borger and El Paso, Texas, connecting midcontinent refining with Southwest demand centers, and will include reversing the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to route midcontinent products into Western Gateway.Once complete, the initiative is designed to create a robust transport corridor linking Midwest refining with growth markets in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas, via the CALNEV extension. The project seeks to diversify supply, improve energy security, and support sustained demand growth across the western United States.

value.Title: Phibro Animal Health Corporation Launches Companywide Sustainable Solutions Platform; Introduces VERRATAIN(TM) Verified Sustainability Solutions Through Strategic VAXA Technologies Partnership

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-W0J3-S3B1-247P-00000-00Phibro Animal Health Corporation Launches Companywide Sustainable Solutions Platform; Introduces VERRATAIN(TM) Verified Sustainability Solutions Through Strategic VAXA Technologies Partnership2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:00:05ZLexisNexisPhibro Animal Health Corporation Launches Companywide Sustainable Solutions Platform; Introduces VERRATAIN(TM) Verified Sustainability Solutions Through Strategic VAXA Technologies PartnershipTEANECK, N.J.

Phibro Animal Health Corporation (Nasdaq: PAHC) today announced the launch of its new Sustainable Solutions Platform (SSP), a companywide initiative designed to help animal protein, dairy and petfood producers address greenhouse gas emissions through practical, science-based solutions that integrate seamlessly into existing feed and production systems.

As part of the SSP launch, Phibro is introducing Verratain(TM) Verified Sustainability Solutions, the Company's first master brand for sustainable products that can significantly lower emissions across animal protein and petfood supply chains. The initial Verratain product line is being launched through a strategic partnership with VAXA Technologies and is based on VAXA Technologies' low carbon, high value microalgae feed materials that enable efficient supply chain decarbonization.

The first two products under the Verratain brand are Verratain(TM) Spirulina and Verratain(TM) Omega Oil, which are low-carbon microalgae feed ingredients. When these products are incorporated into the animal's diet as a nutrition source, producers can materially reduce the carbon intensity of animal feed to allow organizations to meet their sustainability goals while supporting animal health. "Our Verratain Verified Sustainability Solutions represent a natural evolution of Phibro's portfolio as our customers increasingly seek credible, scalable sustainability solutions," said Dani Bendheim, incoming Chief Executive Officer of Phibro Animal Health Corporation. "These offerings complement our existing animal health and nutrition solutions while directly addressing the evolving sustainability needs of animal protein and petfood producers and their downstream customers."

Targeting a Major Scope 3 Emissions Hotspot

The World Benchmarking Alliance reports that 165 of the world's 350 most influential food and agriculture companies have disclosed Scope 3 climate commitments, which typically call for 20-30% reductions in value-chain emissions by 2030. At scale, this implies an estimated $80-300 billion per-year decarbonization value signal under SBTi-aligned internal carbon pricing. (Source: World Business Council for Sustainable Development. 2024. Scope 3 Navigator for Agri-Food. Available: https://cecodes.org.co/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/WBCSD-AF-C-suite-Scope-3-Navigator-deck.pdf Accessed: May 1, 2026).

Feed is typically the largest source of Scope 3 emissions for livestock, poultry and aquaculture producers. By focusing on feeding a low carbon alternative nutrient source, Verratain directly targets one of the most significant emissions drivers in animal protein supply chains.

Verratain Spirulina and Verratain Omega Oil are produced by VAXA Technologies in their proprietary state of the art indoor cultivation platform directly connected to renewable geothermal energy, clean water resources, and controlled growing conditions. The production process yields low-carbon feed material that is designed to seamlessly integrate with existing feeding systems. The program utilizes life cycle assessment-based solutions to create effective Scope 3 decarbonization which is incorporated into the product's life-cycle assessments, in accordance with leading international standards such as the Greenhouse Gas Protocol.

Leadership to Build and Scale the Platform

To lead the Sustainable Solutions Platform and the Verratain portfolio, Phibro recently appointed Peter Bunce as Head of Sustainable Solutions. Bunce brings more than two decades of experience in agricultural sustainability and voluntary carbon markets, including building large-scale programs that connect farmers with downstream consumer packaged goods companies and retailers.

"I joined Phibro because of its deep integration across global protein and dairy supply chains, where scalable solutions can deliver meaningful emissions reductions," said Bunce. "After many years working in agricultural voluntary emissions markets, I have rarely seen innovations that combine impact and practicality like Verratain."

Advancing a More Efficient and Resilient Food System

The launch of the Verratain product line marks a pivotal commercial milestone for Phibro's sustainability initiatives. The Company anticipates the commencement of initial sales within the coming months, providing early adopters with a first-to-market opportunity to integrate these low-carbon microalgae solutions into their production systems.

Looking ahead, Phibro views the Sustainable Solutions Platform as a long-term growth business that will expand beyond the initial products and partnership. To learn more about Verratain and our science-based solutions or to schedule a call with our sustainability team, please visit us at www.pahc.com/verratain or contact us at verratain@pahc.com .

About VAXA Technologies

VAXA is a food tech / climate tech company that has successfully industrialized its patented, carbon-negative microalgae production platform, to deliver proven, scalable solutions. By leveraging renewable energy, waste streams, and machine learning, VAXA produces high-value ingredients that physically remove emissions from the global supply chain, allowing partners to decarbonize at the source. Unlike traditional, resource-heavy algae production, VAXA's modular, indoor facility in Iceland achieves a 100x higher decarbonization impact using less than 1% of the resources required by conventional methods, utilizing a bio secured optimized environment to ensure a consistent, food-grade output that provides a reliable, superior supply chain for corporations looking to improve their products and meet their sustainability goals.

For more information on VAXA visit www.vaxa.life / www.vaxaimpact.com .

About Phibro Animal Health Corporation

Phibro Animal Health Corporation is a leading global diversified animal health and nutrition company. The Company strives to be a trusted partner to livestock producers, farmers, veterinarians, and consumers who raise or care for farm and companion animals by providing solutions that help maintain and enhance animal health. For more information, visit www.pahc.com .

urn:contentItem:6JH4-BNG3-S9G3-61M2-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ AUSTRALIAN WEALTH MANAGER PARTNERS WITH ORACLE AI TO BOOST EFFICIENCY

Colonial First State (CFS) is investing in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to transform its operations. The Australian wealth manager manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly a million Australians as of the 24th of March 2026.The move consolidates HR, ERP and EPM into a single, unified platform, driving streamlined processes and data-driven decision-making.Jeroen Buwalda, COO, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The rollout is expected to accelerate workflows and support strategic initiatives.Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, noted that CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving wealth-management landscape. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ STRATEGIC BANKING MERGER APPROVED: 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP AND MID PENN BANCORP

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026, with more than 80% of outstanding shares represented and over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Rory G. Ritrievi commented that the support for the transaction was tremendous, and 1st Colonial's Robert White said the merger will strengthen the bank's ability to serve customers and communities.The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and bolster its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey. After closing, Mid Penn is expected to reach about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026, with 59 retail locations in Harrisburg-based operations.The deal reflects ongoing consolidation in the regional banking sector as both banks prepare for the integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66, KINDER MORGAN BUILD WESTERN GATEWAY FUEL PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER PLEDGES

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The project targets a new cross-regional fuel corridor to alleviate reliance on limited California pipeline links.The plan would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets and will tie into Las Vegas through CALNEV. It contemplates constructing a Borger, Texas to Phoenix line and reversing the Colton, California to Phoenix segment to enable east-to-west transport into California. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

+ BACKLOG-DRIVEN GROWTH AT KINDER MORGAN INTERSECTS WITH INSIDER SALES CONCERNS

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan’s US$10 billion backlog, centered on natural gas, is positioned to benefit from AI-enabled data centers and LNG exports.The company reported Q4 2025 results with record net income and adjusted EBITDA, noting that about 96% of cash flows come from fee-based or hedged contracts.Insider activity has continued, with a senior terminal executive selling shares over the past year, while the company targets US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion in earnings by 2029, a roughly 3.9% annual growth trajectory subject to market conditions and asset-management execution.Fair-value estimates among analysts range from about US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, reflecting divergent views on the backlog's ability to translate into value.

+ WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE GAINS MOMENTUM, LINKING MIDWEST REFINERIES TO WESTERN MARKETS

The initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline has closed with strong market interest, and Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan say the response supports advancing the project. A second open season is planned for January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and expanded delivery options, including new destinations west of Colton through a joint tariff with SFPP, L.P. that reconfigures the Colton-Phoenix segment to support westbound flows.The Western Gateway includes a new Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona line and the reversal of the SFPP, L.P. Colton-Phoenix segment. The plan also reverses the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to feed midcontinent refineries and supply Western Gateway’s western deliveries.The project aims to connect Midwest refining capacity with growing demand in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing supply diversity and energy security across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: COLONIAL GROUP INC. - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND PEERS
